biopsyNotes: A. Biopsy Clinical Diagnosis: SCC Location: Right Temple Method: Shave The patient gave informed consent for this procedure. After consent was obtained, attention was turned toward the area of interest which was disinfected and cleaned with alcohol. Local anesthetic, 2% lidocaine with epinephrine was infiltrated around the area of interest. The superficial layers of the site were removed by Shave method using a Blue Dermablade. The specimen was placed in a bottle labeled A. #RA15-147. Pressure was applied to achieve hemostasis. Bacitracin was applied at the wound site and it was bandaged. Patient tolerated the procedure well and wound care instructions were reviewed and given to the patient.

A. Biopsy
Clinical Diagnosis: r/o LM
Location: Right Cheek
Method: Shave
 The patient gave informed consent for this procedure. After consent was obtained, attention was turned toward the area of interest which was disinfected and cleaned with alcohol. Local anesthetic, 2% lidocaine with epinephrine was infiltrated around the area of interest. The superficial layers of the site were removed by Shave method using a Number 15. The specimen was placed in a bottle labeled A. #RA26-63.
Pressure was applied to achieve hemostasis. Aquaphor was applied at the wound site and it was bandaged. Patient tolerated the procedure well and wound care instructions were reviewed and given to the patient.

assesment:
Neoplasm Uncertain Behavior of the Skin
Management options were discussed, including: biopsy, confocal microscopy, watchful waiting. Risks and benefits of all options were reviewed.
Patient opted to proceed with the following treatment:
Actinic Keratosis
 Discussed with patient that these are considered to be precancerous and are primarily caused by long-term exposure to ultraviolet (UV) radiation from the sun but can be caused from other sources. Management options were discussed, including: cryotherapy, topical therapies (5FU, imiquimod, etc.). Risks and benefits of each reviewed, with the primary goal of treatment to prevent the progression of the lesion. Patient educated on the importance of sun protection.
Patient opted to proceed with the following treatment: 
 
Seborrheic Keratosis - Inflamed
 Considering the patient’s history and examination, the diagnosis is most
 consistent with inflamed seborrheic keratosis with pruritus. 
Management options discussed with patient: cryotherapy vs. surgical removal. Risks and benefits of all management options were discussed.
Patient opted to proceed with the following:
